summary: Engine Comparison Notebook — Full Calibration Comparison ========================================================= Paste each cell (# %%) into Jupyter.  Strategy to avoid re-downloading the NC file 3 times:   1. Satpy extracts first → NC file cached in cmp_satpy/   2. Rasterio extractions point to the SAME nc file via symlink      (TIF filenames are suffixed per calibration to avoid clashes)

Run a single GOES extraction from a SearchResult.  Usage:     # From search results parquet:     python development/local/run_extraction.py results.parquet      # Or configure manually and run:     python development/local/run_extraction.py

Side-by-side comparison: satpy nearest-neighbor vs LUT crop extraction.  Shows that the LUT extraction is pixel-perfect vs pyresample's get_sample_from_neighbour_info (ground truth), while satpy produces slightly different results because it uses a different source geometry (SwathDefinition with per-pixel lon/lat vs AreaDefinition regular grid).  Usage:     uv run python3 development/local/compare_satpy_lut.py
Diagnose the coordinate mapping between LUT and cell pixels.

Benchmark: LUT engine vs rasterio engine for GOES extraction.  Usage:     python development/local/benchmark_lut_engine.py \         --goes-file development/local/OR_ABI-L1b-RadF-*.nc \         --lut-dir development/local/luts_zarr/ \         --resolution 1000 \         --num-cells 10  Compares: 1. engine='rasterio' (current approach - reproj per cell) 2. engine='lut' (new approach - index slicing)
Benchmark: satpy engine vs rasterio/GDAL engine for GOES extraction.  Usage:     uv run python development/local/benchmark_engines.py

Generate all GOES LUTs for production use.  Downloads one sample C01 file per satellite/product combo and generates  all intersecting UTM zone LUTs at 500m, 1000m, 2000m resolution in parallel.  Combos:   - GOES-19 Full Disk  (G19_RadF)   - GOES-19 CONUS      (G19_RadC)   - GOES-18 Full Disk  (G18_RadF)   - GOES-18 CONUS      (G18_RadC)  Usage:     python development/local/generate_all_luts.py [--output-dir luts/] [--max-workers 8]

Minimal end-to-end example of crop-based GOES LUT extraction.  Shows how to: 1. Load a UTM zone LUT 2. Compute the minimal GOES source crop for that zone 3. Read only that crop via h5py (avoids loading 117M pixels) 4. Extract a single 100×100 grid cell from the crop  Run with:     uv run python3 development/local/plot_crop_example.py
